tasks.title: Retrieve relevant documents and policies on Booking.com cancellation and charges
Search latest customer complaints and Booking.com responses related to cancellation charges
Analyze case details and Booking.com policies to determine if charge was valid
Formulate resolution steps including refund or escalation if charge was improper
Document findings and communicate resolution to customer support team

rag_output.summary:
- If the guest cancelled within the free cancellation window and was not marked as a no-show, the charge should be treated as potentially refundable; support must gather the cancellation timestamp, property policy, and payment owner before responding [1].
- For non-refundable bookings, refunds are generally not promised unless there is duplicate charging, a property error, or inconsistency in the policy shown at booking [2].
- It is crucial to determine who collected the payment (Booking.com or the property); if the property charged directly, support should obtain the payment receipt, property communication, and cancellation timeline before proceeding [3].

Sources: [1] refunds_policy.md, [2] refunds_policy.md, [3] payment_disputes.md

evidence.title: refunds_policy.md
evidence.excerpt: If the guest cancelled inside the free-cancellation window and the booking was not later marked as a no-show, support should treat the charge as potentially refundable and gather the cancellation timestamp, property policy, and payment owner before responding.

evidence.title: refunds_policy.md
evidence.excerpt: If the booking is non-refundable, support should not promise a refund. The case can still be reviewed if there is duplicate charging, a property-side error, or evidence that the policy shown at booking was inconsistent.

evidence.title: payment_disputes.md
evidence.excerpt: The first question is who collected the money: Booking.com or the property. If the property charged the guest directly, support should collect the payment receipt, property message trail, and cancellation timeline before deciding on next steps.

web_output.summary:
Task: Explain Booking.com's Terms and Partner Interface FAQs

Public evidence:
- Booking.com provides a Partner Interface (TPI) FAQ that details how partners can integrate and manage their listings, reservations, and content on the platform [1].
- The Terms and Conditions page outlines the contractual obligations, rights, and responsibilities of partners using Booking.com services, including content accuracy, payment terms, and cancellation policies [2].
- Both resources emphasize compliance with Booking.com's standards to ensure quality and reliability for users and partners alike [1][2].

Sources: [1] https://www.booking.com/tpi_faq.html, [2] https://www.booking.com/content/terms.html

action_plan:
- Verify the guest’s cancellation timestamp to confirm it was within the free cancellation window and ensure the booking was not marked as a no-show; gather the property’s cancellation policy and identify who processed the payment (Booking.com or the property).  
- If the property charged directly, request the payment receipt, any communication from the property regarding the charge, and the full cancellation timeline to assess the validity of the charge.  
- Based on collected evidence, if the charge was made despite a valid free cancellation, initiate a refund process or escalate to the appropriate team, referencing Booking.com’s refund policies and partner obligations; communicate clearly with the customer about the findings and next steps.
next_steps:
1. Request and verify the guest’s cancellation timestamp and confirm it falls within the free cancellation window; check that the booking was not marked as a no-show.  
2. Obtain the property’s cancellation policy and determine who processed the payment (Booking.com or the property).  
3. If the property charged directly, ask for the payment receipt, any related property communications, and the full cancellation timeline for review.  
4. Based on gathered evidence, decide whether to initiate a refund or escalate to the relevant team, and communicate the findings and next steps clearly to the customer.
final_answer:
Issue:  
The customer cancelled their booking within the free cancellation window but was still charged for the reservation.

Internal policy:  
If a guest cancels within the free cancellation window and the booking is not marked as a no-show, the charge is potentially refundable. Support must verify the cancellation timestamp, review the property’s cancellation policy, and identify who processed the payment (Booking.com or the property). For payments collected directly by the property, additional documentation such as the payment receipt and property communications must be obtained before proceeding. Refunds are generally not issued for non-refundable bookings unless there is evidence of duplicate charges, property errors, or policy inconsistencies.

Public policy:  
Booking.com’s Terms and Partner Interface FAQs outline the responsibilities and obligations of partners, including accurate representation of cancellation policies and payment handling. These resources emphasize compliance with Booking.com standards to ensure transparency and reliability for customers and partners alike.

Recommended action:  
1. Confirm the guest’s cancellation timestamp to ensure it falls within the free cancellation window and verify the booking was not marked as a no-show.  
2. Obtain the property’s cancellation policy and determine who processed the payment.  
3. If the property charged directly, request the payment receipt, any relevant property communications, and the full cancellation timeline.  
4. Assess the evidence to determine if the charge was valid; if not, initiate a refund or escalate the case to the appropriate team.  
5. Communicate clearly with the customer about the findings, the refund status, and any next steps.

Next steps:  
- Contact the customer to confirm the cancellation timestamp and gather any relevant details.  
- Reach out to the property to obtain their cancellation policy and payment information.  
- Collect necessary documentation if the property processed the payment directly.  
- Review all information to decide on refund eligibility or escalation.  
- Provide the customer with a clear update and resolution timeline.

Cited Sources:
- https://partner.booking.com/en-us/help/reservations/reduce-cancellations/handling-cancellation-fees-and-commission
- https://www.booking.com/content/terms.html
- https://www.booking.com/tpi_faq.html
